phase: B9 Schema standardization and compatibility / B10 operational feedback

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map component/relation/signature fields by stable camelCase names; adapter coverageBoundary, unsupportedConstructs, missingEvidence, and nonConclusions must remain explicit.
newRequiredAssumptions: New required axes must declare measuredZero/measuredNonzero/unmeasured/outOfScope semantics.
New adapter evidence must preserve coverage and missing-evidence boundaries.
coverageExactnessBoundary: metricStatus is the compatibility boundary for coverage and exactness of Sig0 axes.
Sig0 is bounded adapter evidence, not the ArchSig primary review surface or extractor completeness proof.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map ids, relation refs, evidence refs, claims, coverage layers, and theorem-package refs explicitly; never infer a formal claim from renamed fields.
newRequiredAssumptions: New required theorem, coverage, projection, or exactness assumptions must be listed in claims or coverage layers.
coverageExactnessBoundary: AIR coverage layers and claim classifications are the exactness boundary for report generation.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map source inventory, prompt/model provenance, atom candidates, molecule candidates, obstruction circuit candidates, observation gaps, AAT-facing and SFT-facing map items, coverage, conflicts, and non-conclusions by stable camelCase names.
newRequiredAssumptions: New mapping kinds must preserve source refs, claim boundary, missing evidence, and non-conclusions.
New atomic observation fields must remain optional/default-compatible in archmap-v0 unless schemaVersion is deliberately bumped.
SFT-facing mapping kinds must remain projection input and not forecast result claims.
coverageExactnessBoundary: ArchMap is supplied JSON evidence, not architecture ground truth, certified ArchitectureAtom truth, or a Lean theorem claim.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Keep source inventory checks, source ref checks, claim boundary checks, semantic coverage checks, conflict checks, projection-separation checks, formal promotion guardrail checks, homomorphism diagnostics, and atomic observation summary separate.
newRequiredAssumptions: New checks must report whether they fail, warn, or pass without promoting uncertain mappings.
Atomic observation checks must distinguish atom candidates, molecule candidates, obstruction circuit candidates, and observation gaps.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Validation pass does not imply semantic correctness, completeness, architecture lawfulness, certified atom truth, zero curvature, or SFT forecast correctness.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map adopted laws, layer selectors, dependency rules, exceptions, and SRP taxonomy explicitly; do not infer policy from repository shape.
newRequiredAssumptions: New law families must declare deterministic versus LLM-review enforcement boundary.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Architecture policy is review evidence, not a Lean theorem or architecture lawfulness proof.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Keep identity, adopted law, layer selector, dependency rule, SRP evidence, and non-conclusion checks separate.
newRequiredAssumptions: New checks must distinguish fail, warn, and unmeasured policy evidence.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Validation pass does not imply architecture lawfulness or SRP violation.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map deterministic Layered findings, allowed exceptions, SRP cues, unmeasured selectors, review actions, and non-conclusions explicitly.
newRequiredAssumptions: New law findings must preserve evidence refs and policy refs before review classification.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Layered findings are selected-universe tooling evidence and SRP cues are not tool-only violations.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map source inventory refs, prompt pack refs, model provenance, required workflow, and private / unavailable generation boundary explicitly.
newRequiredAssumptions: New generation workflow fields must preserve model provenance and validation boundary.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Generation protocol does not execute the model or prove the generated ArchMap correct.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map ArchMap, AIR, theorem-check, feature-report, and policy-decision refs to reviewer-facing cues and missing evidence without automatic merge approval.
newRequiredAssumptions: New cue kinds must declare evidence boundary and avoid merge-decision or architecture-lawfulness claims.
coverageExactnessBoundary: PR quality analysis is review evidence, not merge approval or a global architecture ranking.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map AAT concepts, source refs, selected universe, witness refs, operation candidates, theorem boundaries, review actions, and responsibility splits without promoting tooling evidence to proof.
newRequiredAssumptions: New AAT concept mappings must declare expressibility, retention, reviewability, measurement status, responsibility, and non-conclusions.
New measured axes must keep measuredZero, measuredNonzero, unmeasured, outOfScope, private, unavailable, unsupported, and dynamic boundaries distinct.
coverageExactnessBoundary: AAT Observable Bundle is a review artifact bundle, not AAT itself, extractor completeness, global lawfulness proof, or merge approval.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map review summary, witness, theorem precondition, coverage gap, repair suggestion, and non-conclusion fields separately.
newRequiredAssumptions: New required review assumptions must appear in discharged/undischarged assumptions or theorem precondition checks.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Coverage gaps, runtime summary, semantic summary, and non-conclusions delimit report exactness.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map witness id, layer, kind, extension role, classification, evidence, theorem refs, claim classification, measurement boundary, and non-conclusions independently.
newRequiredAssumptions: New witness kinds must declare layer, evidence requirements, measurement boundary, and non-conclusions.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Witness measurement boundary and claim classification distinguish measured evidence from formal theorem claims.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map stable grouping keys, aggregation window, source, metric, status, suppression, repair, witness, claim, and non-conclusion fields separately.
newRequiredAssumptions: New batch metrics must declare grouping key, aggregation window, measurement boundary, and evidence refs.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Ledger status and suppression metadata are operational state, not formal proof state.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

compatibilityBoundary.fieldMappingPolicy: Map axis id, layer, value type, measurement boundary, evidence requirements, theorem refs, and non-conclusions explicitly.
newRequiredAssumptions: New reported axes must declare whether unmeasured differs from measured zero.
coverageExactnessBoundary: Axis coverage and exactness metadata delimit what a zero or nonzero value can support.
nonConclusions: field mapping does not prove semantic preservation
missing coverage is not measured zero
renamed fields do not discharge new required assumptions

dimensions.checkerBoundary: A checker may validate declared field mappings, but it must not infer semantic equivalence.
Deprecated fields remain compatibility metadata until removal; migration must preserve explicit reader behavior.
New assumptions must be reported as missing or undischarged unless explicit evidence is present.
Non-conclusions are mandatory guardrails and cannot be dropped by migration.
Coverage and exactness metadata delimit what any reported value can support.

requiredChecks: field mapping entries are explicit for renamed or removed fields
deprecated fields declare replacement or removal behavior
new required assumptions are surfaced as missing until discharged
coverage and exactness metadata are not dropped
nonConclusions: compatibility policy is not a proof of semantic preservation
compatibility policy is not evidence of extractor completeness
compatibility policy is not a Lean theorem package
schema migration is not a semantic-preservation theorem
catalog membership does not imply extractor completeness
compatibility pass does not imply architecture lawfulness
compatibility pass does not promote tooling evidence to a Lean theorem claim
